Try LightGBM with lambda_l2=8.0, lambda_l1=1.0, min_child_samples=250, min_gain_to_split=0.05, but increase max_depth back to 8 and num_leaves=256. Also increase feature_fraction to 0.7 and bagging_fraction to 0.8 to allow more signal while keeping regularization strong. Best was exp_0009 at 0.94340 — testing if slightly more tree capacity with same regularization helps.

Try LightGBM with num_leaves=384, max_depth=8, learning_rate=0.035, lambda_l2=8.0, lambda_l1=1.0, min_child_samples=200, min_gain_to_split=0.03. Best so far is exp_0009 at 0.94340 with lambda_l2=8.0, min_child_samples=250, max_depth=7. Hypothesis: slightly more capacity (more leaves, deeper

Try Tweedie variance power=1.3 combined with num_leaves=255 and learning_rate=0.035. Best so far is exp_0009 at 0.94340 with tweedie_variance_power=1.5. Tweedie 1.2 was tried in exp_0001 and was worse (0.94482

Try LightGBM with num_leaves=512, learning_rate=0.025, max_depth=9, lambda_l2=8.0, lambda_l1=1.0, min_child_samples=250, min_gain_to_split=0.05. Best so far is exp_0009 at 0.94340 with num_leaves=256, max_depth=7. Hypothesis: more leaves with slower learning rate may capture finer patterns while regularization prevents overfitting.
